seed_problem: Place watchtowers on a forest grid to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total watchtower cost; checker verifies full coverage and scores baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
mutation: composite: change the goal from min-#facilities k-center covering to MIN-TOTAL-COST radius-R covering (weighted radius-R dominating set), and generalize the input from a uniform grid to a weighted channel graph with parallel edges and long shortcuts, so optimal covering is NP-hard and greedy-vs-LP-rounding-vs-local-search strategies diverge.
title: Tide Pool Watch: Minimum-Cost Sensor Cover
strategies: everywhere baseline: install a sensor in every pool (feasible, matches the checker baseline cost)
coverage-only greedy: repeatedly install where it newly monitors the most uncovered pools, ignoring cost
cost-effective greedy: repeatedly install where newly-covered-per-cost is maximal, then prune redundant sensors
randomized multi-restart local search: perturb the cost-effective greedy order across restarts, prune, and keep the lowest-cost feasible cover
